by_judge.judge: C M POONACHA WILL SIT IN COURT HALL NO.6 AT DHARWAD
D K SINGH & VENKATESH NAIK T

narrative:
On July 10, 2026, the Karnataka High Court recorded an overall hearing rate of 45.4%, with 926 cases heard out of 2,038 scheduled across 66 judges. This conversion rate reflects a minor decrease from the 45.9% recorded on June 25, 2026, but remains slightly above the 44.0% clearance rate observed on June 18, 2026. 

Judge-wise performance showed significant variability under high caseloads. Justice S. Vishwajith Shetty in Hall 19 managed the day's largest docket, hearing 61 out of 151 scheduled matters (40.4%). Meanwhile, Justice V. Srishananda achieved a strong 86.0% hearing rate by clearing 37 of 43 scheduled cases. In contrast, exceptionally heavy dockets in other halls faced backlogs; Justice B. M. Shyam Prasad in Hall 6 heard only 13 of his primary list of 101 scheduled cases (12.9%), and Justice Ashok S. Kinagi in Hall 9 cleared just 13 of 99 scheduled cases (13.1%).

In terms of hearing stages, the court prioritised "Orders", which constituted the largest block with 762 cases scheduled and 391 heard (51.3%), representing an increased conversion efficiency compared to the 48.4% recorded for Orders on June 25. "Preliminary Hearings" converted at 48.3% (128 of 265), while "Final Hearings" lagged behind with only 78 of 337 matters heard (23.1%). 

Case type distribution was heavily led by Writ Petitions (WP), with 382 heard out of 805 scheduled (47.5%), representing a slight improvement from the 46.3% WP hearing rate on June 25. Miscellaneous First Appeals (MFA) also demonstrated strong progress with a 51.0% clearance rate (174 of 341 scheduled), whereas Criminal Petitions (CRL.P) saw a lower conversion rate of 26.8% (48 of 179 scheduled).
